glyphs.label: Visualizing Network Paths, Delay, and Packet Loss through TTL and ICMP

- Discovers network hops between source and destination.
- Identifies delay and packet loss at each hop.
- Troubleshoots routing issues, latency, and network congestion.

- TTL (Time To Live) in the IP header decreases by 1 at each router hop.
- When TTL reaches 0, the router drops the packet and sends ICMP Time Exceeded back to the source.

Linux traceroute default:
high unused ports.

- Sends three probes per hop to measure packet delay.
- Round-Trip Time is displayed in milliseconds (ms).

Step 1: User runs tracert google.com
(e.g., C:\> tracert google.com)

Step 2: Source sends packet with TTL=1;
Gateway drops it and returns ICMP Time Exceeded.

Step 3: Source sends packet with TTL=2;
ISP router drops it and returns ICMP Time Exceeded.

Step 4: TTL increases hop-by-hop,
discovering subsequent routers.

Step 5: Packet reaches destination;
server returns Echo Reply or Port Unreachable.
